info.name: File Upload Service — Complete API
info.description:
# File Upload Microservice — Postman Collection

## Quick Start
1. Import this collection into Postman
2. No environment setup needed — all defaults are in collection variables
3. Run **Upload Files → Single File Upload** first — `fileId` is auto-saved to collection variables
4. All subsequent requests use `{{fileId}}` automatically

## Collection Variables
| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `baseUrl` | http://localhost:4001 | Service base URL |
| `tenantId` | tenant-a | Primary test tenant |
| `tenantB` | tenant-b | Secondary tenant (cross-tenant tests) |
| `userId` | user-001 | User ID via X-User-Id header |
| `userRole` | admin | Role via X-User-Role header |
| `fileId` | *(auto-set)* | Populated after first upload |

## Response Envelope
```
// Success
{ "success": true, "statusCode": 200, "message": "...", "data": {...} }

// Error
{ "success": false, "statusCode": 400, "message": "...", "error": { "code": "...", "errors": [...] } }
```

variable.description: Auto-populated by the Single File Upload request

request.description: Returns service status, DB connectivity state, and current timestamp. No headers required.

item.name: 📤 Upload Files
item.description: All upload requests use multipart/form-data. The `files` field accepts up to 10 files. After a successful upload the test script auto-saves the first file's ID into the `fileId` collection variable.

exec: pm.test('File data returned and fileId saved', () => {

request.description:
Upload a single file. Optional fields: `category`, `description`, `title`, `altText`, `author`, `language`, `isPublic`, `linkedEntityType`, `linkedEntityId`, `tags` (repeated), `custom` (JSON string).

**fileId is auto-saved to collection variables after success.**
Upload up to 10 files in a single request using multiple `files` fields. Max 10 files per request (LIMIT_FILE_COUNT enforced).
Send a multipart/form-data request with no file attached. Expected: `400 Bad Request` with message `No files provided`.

exec: pm.test('Status 201 — falls back to default tenant', () => pm.response.to.have.status(201));

request.description: Omit `X-Tenant-Id`. The service falls back to `DEFAULT_TENANT_ID` (default: `"default"`) — the upload succeeds and the file is stored under that tenant.

formdata.description: Select a file larger than MAX_FILE_SIZE (default: 10MB)

request.description:
Upload a file exceeding `MAX_FILE_SIZE` (default 10 MB). Multer throws `LIMIT_FILE_SIZE` → error handler returns `413 Payload Too Large`.

To test: select any file > 10 MB, or lower `MAX_FILE_SIZE=1024` in `.env` and retry.

formdata.description: Select a .exe, .zip, or other blocked file type

request.description: Upload a file with a MIME type not in `ALLOWED_MIME_TYPES`. Default allowed: `image/jpeg, image/png, image/gif, application/pdf`. Try uploading a `.exe` or `.zip`.
Use `file` (singular) instead of `files` (plural). Multer throws `LIMIT_UNEXPECTED_FILE` → `400 Bad Request` with guidance message.

item.name: 📋 List & Search Files
item.description: All list requests are scoped to the tenant in X-Tenant-Id. Supports filtering, sorting, and pagination.

request.description: Returns all active files for the tenant. Default page=1, limit=20, sorted by createdAt desc.
Full-text search across `originalName` and `metadata.description` (case-insensitive regex).
Filter results to a specific MIME type.
Filter by one or more tags (OR logic — files matching any of the tags are returned).
Filter files uploaded within a date range. Both dates are ISO 8601 format.

query.description: Prefix - for descending. Fields: originalName, size, createdAt, updatedAt

request.description: Paginate results with 5 per page, sorted newest first. Sort ascending: `sort=createdAt`. Sort by size: `sort=-size`.
Return only files uploaded by a specific user.
Return only files with a specific category label.
Return only files with the specified language code.
Return only files matching the visibility flag.
Return all files linked to a specific entity. Both params can be used independently or together.
Use a disallowed sort field. Joi validation returns `400`. Allowed fields: `originalName`, `size`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`.
Fetch full file metadata by ID. Request is scoped to the tenant — a file from another tenant returns 404.
Valid ObjectId format but no matching document. Returns `404 NOT_FOUND`.
Pass a non-ObjectId string. Mongoose throws a CastError → error handler returns `400` with message `Invalid file ID: 'not-a-valid-mongo-id' is not a valid ID`.

exec: pm.test('File not visible to wrong tenant', () => {

header.description: Using TENANT-B to look up a TENANT-A file

request.description: Use `X-Tenant-Id: tenant-b` to request a file that belongs to `tenant-a`. The query includes `tenantId` in the filter, so the document is invisible and a `404` is returned. **Multi-tenancy isolation is enforced at the DB query level.**
Streams the file with `Content-Disposition: attachment`. The browser will prompt a file save dialog.
Use `?inline=1` to stream with `Content-Disposition: inline`. Images and PDFs will render in the browser.

exec: pm.test('Redirect or OK (cloud storage redirects, local returns 200)', () => {

query.description: Generates a time-limited signed URL and redirects to it

request.description: For cloud storage (S3, GCS, R2, Azure): generates a pre-signed URL and redirects (302). For local storage: streams directly (200). Signed URL expiry is controlled by `SIGNED_URL_EXPIRY` env (default: 3600 seconds).
Attempt to download a non-existent file ID.

item.name: ✏️ Rename File
item.description: PATCH /:id/rename — Updates only the display name (originalName) of a file. The storage key on disk/cloud is unchanged.

request.description: Renames a file's display name. Only `originalName` is updated — the actual storage key (path on disk/cloud) stays unchanged. A `rename` transaction is logged.
Send empty body. Joi requires `name` field → `400 Validation Error`.
Name exceeds 255 characters → Joi validation returns `400`.
Rename a non-existent file ID.

item.name: 📝 Update Metadata
item.description: PATCH /:id — Updates file metadata fields: originalName, description, tags, custom. At least one field must be provided.

request.description: Update `metadata.description`, `metadata.tags`, and `metadata.custom`. An `update_metadata` transaction is logged.
Update just the display name via the metadata endpoint (alternative to the dedicated `/rename` endpoint).
Joi schema requires at least 1 key (`.min(1)`) → `400` on empty body.
Update metadata of a non-existent file ID.

item.name: 🔄 Replace File Content
item.description: PUT /:id/replace — Replaces the binary content of an existing file. The old version is saved into the `versions` array on the File document. Form-data key must be `file` (singular).

formdata.description: New file content (key must be 'file' singular)

request.description: Replaces the file binary. The old storage key is preserved in `versions[]` for rollback. **Field name must be `file` (singular)**, not `files`.
Send multipart request with no file attached → `400 No file provided`.
Use `files` (plural) instead of `file` for the replace endpoint. Multer throws `LIMIT_UNEXPECTED_FILE` → `400`.

item.name: 🗑️ Delete Files
item.description: Soft delete marks the file as `status: deleted` (recoverable). Permanent delete removes the DB record AND the storage object.

exec: console.log('File soft-deleted. Run upload again to get a new fileId.');

request.description: Soft-deletes the file (`status: 'deleted'`). The record remains in MongoDB and the storage object is NOT removed. A `delete` transaction is logged. The file will no longer appear in list or get queries.

exec: console.log('File permanently deleted. Upload a new file to continue testing.');

request.description: Permanently removes the MongoDB document AND the file from storage (disk/S3/GCS/etc). All version storage keys are also removed. **This is irreversible.**
Delete a non-existent file ID.

item.name: 📜 File Transactions (Audit Log)
item.description: Every upload, rename, update, replace, and delete operation creates a transaction record. These are immutable audit-trail entries.

request.description: Returns the full audit trail for a file, sorted newest first. Each entry records: `operation` (upload / rename / update_metadata / replace / delete / permanent_delete), `status` (success / failed / pending), `performedBy`, `requestId`, `payload`, and timestamps.
Transactions for a non-existent file ID.

item.name: ⚠️ System & Edge-Case Errors
item.description: These requests test the error handling layer directly — route not found, malformed JSON, and rate limit exhaustion.

request.description: Hit a route that doesn't exist. The `notFound` middleware returns `404` with `Route /api/nonexistent-route not found`.
Send malformed JSON. Express body-parser emits `entity.parse.failed` → error handler returns `400` with `Invalid JSON in request body`.

To trigger rate limiting: use Postman Runner to send this request more than `UPLOAD_RATE_LIMIT` times (default: 10) within `UPLOAD_RATE_WINDOW` ms (default: 900000 = 15 min). The `Retry-After` response header tells you when to retry.

To test immediately: set `UPLOAD_RATE_LIMIT=2` in `.env` and restart the service.
